article_title: Comet C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS Surges Past Naked-Eye Threshold as It Races Toward April 19 Perihelion

content_preview.title: Comet C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS Surges Past Naked-Eye Threshold as It Races Toward April 19 Perihelion
content_preview.summary: A 170,000-year visitor from the Oort Cloud has brightened faster than expected, reaching magnitude 4.7 and growing a 10-degree ion tail as it heads for its closest solar approach on April 19.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Comet C/2025 R3 (PanSTARRS), an Oort Cloud visitor on an approximately 170,000-year orbit, has brightened faster than most forecasters anticipated and is now visible to the unaided eye under dark skies. With perihelion on April 19 and its closest Earth approach on April 26, the comet is entering the peak of a viewing window that observers in the Northern Hemisphere should not miss. After this passage, the comet's outbound trajectory is expected to carry it out of the solar system en...

editor_notes.source_verification: NASA APOD VERIFIED via WebFetch: April 19 perihelion, April 25 Earth closest approach, 10-degree ion tail, naked-eye possibility (with uncertainty caveat) all confirmed. Oort Cloud origin not in APOD but standard for non-periodic comets with 170k-year orbits.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Perihelion date, ion tail length, viewing window verified.
